title: Hemorrhoid follow-up

red_flags.text: Supports disease-specific history, bowel habits, bleeding, prolapse, thrombosis, prior interventions, and clinician-performed focused examination.
red_flags.rationale: The exact evaluation section supports interval bleeding, prolapse, discomfort, swelling, hygiene, bowel habits, continence, prior treatment, and clinician-performed focused examination without assuming haemorrhoids cause the symptoms.
red_flags.evidence_extract: Supports disease-specific history, bowel habits, bleeding, prolapse, thrombosis, prior interventions, and clinician-performed focused examination.

source.title: The American Society of Colon and Rectal Surgeons Clinical Practice Guidelines for the Management of Hemorrhoids

treatment_and_management.text: Supports documentation of clinician-reviewed prior treatment, outcomes, complications, and entered follow-up decisions without creating treatment advice.
treatment_and_management.rationale: The exact follow-up context supports prior intervention outcomes, complications, and clinician-entered follow-up without generating office or operative treatment.
treatment_and_management.evidence_extract: Supports documentation of clinician-reviewed prior treatment, outcomes, complications, and entered follow-up decisions without creating treatment advice.

source.title: The American Society of Colon and Rectal Surgeons Clinical Practice Guidelines for the Management of Hemorrhoids

sources_used.title: The American Society of Colon and Rectal Surgeons Clinical Practice Guidelines for the Management of Hemorrhoids
sources_used.jurisdiction: United States; international colorectal evidence requiring UAE adaptation

source_limitations: Bleeding severity, bowel pattern, prolapse grade, consent, chaperone, examination, diagnosis, medicine, procedure, colon evaluation, referral, and interval remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
All prior supported legacy-item mappings were removed because canonical item-level applicability and workflow-specific rationale were not present in explicit repository records.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
